text: The redox-active metal-organic framework (MOF) Fe2(dobpdc) (dobpdc(4-) denotes 4,4'-dioxidobiphenyl-3,3'-dicarboxylate) underwent topotactic oxidative insertion reaction by oxidizing with a variety of weakly coordinating anions, including BF4(-) and PF6(-). The insertion reaction led to only a little shrinkage of framework, and the appearance of broad intervalence charge-transfer band in UV-Vis spectra, indicating good charge transportation. While both metal and ligand-based oxidations were accessible, the former was fully reversible, and more importantly, all of these oxidations proceeded in a stoichiometric manner under both chemical and electrochemical conditions. Investigation on electrochemical properties of the insertion reaction in the framework under nanoconfinement showed strong dependences on the sizes of anions and solvents. On the basis of anion insertion properties of the Fe2(dobpdc), a dual-ion battery was constructed using sodium as the anode electrode. In this cathode, the Fe2(dobpdc) electrode shows high initial reduction potential and a high cycling stability for at least 50 charge/discharge cycles, delivering a maximum specific energy of up to 316 Wh/kg.
